programming-first-paragraph: I started doing competitive programming since high school. I received a Bronze Medal in National Olympiad in Informatics (NOI) in 2015. Then I joined the Competitive Programming Team of Zhejiang University and competed as the team leader of Team Legilimens (2017-2019) and the student co-coach (2018-2021). During my undergraduate life, I won 12 gold medals in International Collegiate Programming Contest (ICPC), China Collegiate Programming Contest (CCPC) series competitions including the very first domestic ICPC Regional Champion of Zhejiang University in 2017. On behalf of Zhejiang University, I participated in ICPC World Finals 2018 (Beijing).
programming-second-paragraph: During my first year in Georgia Tech, I won the Champion of the 45th International Collegiate Programming Contest Southeast USA Regional Contest and Second Runner-up in the 45th International Collegiate Programming Contest North America Championship. I participated in ICPC World Finals 2022 (Luxor) as a two-person team, Days of Future Past, and ranked 13th place.
programming-third-paragraph: I am an International Grandmaster on Codeforces (Max Rating 2735).
programming-fourth-paragraph: I am a member of SUA Programming Competitions Problems Setter Team.
programming-fifth-paragraph: I am the co-founder and co-president of the Universal Cup, which is an organization aiming at serving the competitive programming community, dedicated to provide high quality training resources and host onsite global events. They aim at establish a communication bridge across the industrial, academic, and competitive programming community, creating a community that beyond geography and age. Over 8000 teams from more than 500 affiliations all over the world, registered and participated in our training. The current season is open for registration now. We successfully hosted the 2024 Summer Summit, the 2025 and the 2026 Universal Cup Finals in Cancún, Dongguan and Shanghai.
hall-of-fame-intro: I am honored to have shared part of the competitive programming journey with several teams or individuals, offering some guidance and advice along the way. Their talent and hard work brought honor to themselves, their teams, and their universities, and I am proud to share in that joy. Most importantly, we grew together through the journey, gained friendship and unforgettable memories, and I sincerely wish them all the best in the future.

Others-1: Keystone Wiki (Feel free contact me for the authority)
contact-content: If you have any questions, would like to discuss my research, or are interested in collaboration opportunities, please don't hesitate to reach out. Below, you can find the best ways to contact me.
personal-profile-3: The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen
bio-first-paragraph: I am a research assistant professor at the Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen (CUHK-Shenzhen) and Shenzhen Loop Area Institute (SLAI) starting in September 2025. I received my Ph.D. in the Algorithms & Complexity Group of University of Waterloo. I am fortunate to be advised by Richard Peng. Prior to this, I received my Master at Georgia Institute of Technology and B.Eng (Honors) at Zhejiang University under supervision of Can Wang.
bio-second-paragraph: My research revolves around the design, analysis, and implementation of provably efficient algorithms and data structures, with a focus on graphs. I am currently exploring research involving AI. I am a founding member and coordinator of the newly established SDS CS Theory Group.
bio-third-paragraph: I am highly involved with Competitive Programming activities. Previously, I competed representing Zhejiang University and Georgia Tech in 2017 - 2021, participating in ICPC World Finals 2018 (Beijing), 2022 (Egypt), winning 2 ICPC regional champion titles along with 12 gold medals. I started helping Egyptian Olympiad in Informatics (EOI) since 2023. And I also have served as the chief judge for ICPC Asia regionals, including the 2025 ICPC East-Continent Finals. I am the contest director of the ICPC National Invitational Contest (Shenzhen), the first ever ICPC event in CUHK-Shenzhen. I am also invited as lecturers in events like North American Programming Camp (NAPC). I am the founder and co-president of the Universal Cup.
bio-fourth-paragraph: When not thinking about problems, I enjoy playing table tennis. Currently, I am using Fan Zhendong Super ALC with Dignics 09C on both sides. I am also a travel and landscape photography enthusiast; you can check out my recently built Footprint. Since June 2025, my main camera has been the Sony A7M4; before that, I mainly used an iPhone Pro. More of my work is on Xiaohongshu.
edu-3-school: Chu Kochen Honors College (Joint Program with College of Computer Science and Technology), Zhejiang University
news-1: Our paper "On the Power of Learning-Augmented Search Trees" is accepted by the 42nd International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML 2025).
news-2: [Competitive Programming Students] For students with a strong competitive programming background—such as ICPC World Finals participants, students with outstanding results in ICPC regional contests, or NOI medalists in high school—you are welcome to apply for the Computer Science PhD program co-advised by Prof. Chenhao Ma and me. We admit students on a rolling basis and slots are limited. Even if you do not yet have a strong research background, that is totally fine—we hope to offer excellent competitive programmers one more option. Feel free to reach out to us for inquiries.
recruit1-title: Ph.D. / Research M.Phil. / Research Assistant, CUHK-Shenzhen
recruit1-before-highlight: I am currently recruiting research M.Phil. and Ph.D. students through multiple tracks, and I am also considering hiring research assistants. Our lab currently has two branches, the Algo Branch and the AI Branch; please refer to the research directions listed below for each branch. If you are interested in any of these directions, please contact me via email / QQ / WeChat, etc. A desirable qualification is relevant research experience, or being
recruit1-highlight: a student with a strong competitive programming background (research experience is not required)
recruit1-after-highlight: , such as participating in ICPC World Finals, achieving strong results in ICPC regional contests, or receiving an NOI medal in high school.
recruit2-title: Joint Ph.D. Program with SLAI and CUHK-Shenzhen
recruit2-highlight: Students in this program can access substantial computing resources, a 30-square-meter single room near Hong Kong, and other generous support.
recruit2-body: If you are interested in the joint program and becoming my Ph.D. student, please review the research directions under the AI Branch and contact me proactively. After an interview, I may recommend suitable candidates to the SLAI selection camp and other appropriate selection tracks. If the pre-admission process and collaboration go smoothly, we will then arrange the CUHK-Shenzhen Ph.D. application to complete the admission loop.
recruit1: [Mphi/PhD/Research Assistant] I am currently recruiting research-based Master’s and PhD students through multiple threads, and I am also considering hiring research assistants. Possible research directions include: (1) AI + large-scale data management; (2) AI + data mining and recommendation systems; (3) machine learning + classical algorithms and data structures; (4) graph data mining and analysis algorithms; (5) theoretical computer science. If you are interested in other research topics, we can always discuss whether it is a good fit. If you are interested, please contact me via email / QQ / WeChat, etc.
recruit2: [Joint Program with SLAI] For students interested in applying to the Shenzhen Loop Area Institute (SLAI), or those who have already received a conditional offer, if you would like me to be your advisor, please contact me for details. If it is a good fit, we can arrange admission. In this case, in addition to the research directions listed above, I am currently particularly interested in large reasoning models and diffusion models, among others. However, all research directions are welcome for discussion—especially those related to AI + science and engineering.
news-3: Our paper "Scalable Approximate Biclique Counting over Large Bipartite Graphs" is accepted by the 52nd International Conference on Very Large Data Bases (VLDB 2026).
news-4: Our paper "Efficient Historical Butterfly Counting in Large Temporal Bipartite Networks via Graph Structure‑aware Index" is accepted by the 51st International Conference on Very Large Data Bases (VLDB 2025).
news-5: Our paper "TopKGAT: A Top-K Objective-Driven Architecture for Recommendation" is accepted by the Web Conference 2026 (WWW 2026).
